name: GrowthBook API
what_it_does: GrowthBook's REST API for managing feature flags and A/B experiments in an open-source experimentation platform, available self-hosted or as managed cloud with warehouse-native analytics integration.
use_cases: Creating and managing feature flags and experiment configurations via REST API
Automating experiment lifecycle — starting, stopping, and analyzing A/B tests from agent workflows
Reading experiment results and winning variants for automated feature rollout decisions
Syncing feature flag state with CI/CD pipelines for progressive delivery automation
Managing organization-wide feature flag environments (dev, staging, production) programmatically
not_for: Teams needing real-time flag evaluation at edge without SDK (GrowthBook requires SDK integration for evaluation)
Non-technical product teams expecting a no-code experimentation experience
Organizations requiring enterprise SLA guarantees without self-hosting capacity
best_when: You want open-source A/B testing with warehouse-native analytics (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift) and need full control over data, either self-hosted or via cloud.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed SaaS with minimal ops overhead and don't have a data warehouse — Statsig or LaunchDarkly are better fits.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens scoped to read-only or read-write. Tokens are organization-scoped. Self-hosted deployments can configure additional auth providers. Secret key must be kept server-side.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted OSS is fully free; Cloud free tier: up to 3 seats, unlimited features
pricing.notes: Open-source self-hosted version is fully featured and free. Cloud offers a generous free tier. Enterprise adds SSO, advanced permissions, and dedicated support.

performance.rate_limits: Cloud: 100 requests/minute per token; self-hosted: no enforced limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error bodies containing a message field. Validation errors specify which field failed. Error quality is good for common operations but edge cases can return generic 400 errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Feature flag reads are idempotent. Feature flag creation is not idempotent — duplicate slugs return 400 conflict, which agents can use as a check. Updates by ID are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: SDK evaluation is client-side — REST API manages configuration only, agents must use SDK for actual flag evaluation in application code
Self-hosted deployments require managing the GrowthBook server, MongoDB, and SDK proxy — not zero-ops
Experiment statistical analysis requires connected data warehouse (BigQuery/Snowflake/Redshift) — not built-in event tracking
Feature flag slugs (IDs) are immutable after creation — choose carefully as they appear in code
Environment keys differ per environment — agents must specify the correct environment when reading flag state
Webhook payloads require HMAC signature verification — agents receiving webhooks must implement signature validation

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source codebase enables full security audit. TLS enforced on cloud; self-hosted must configure TLS. Token scopes limited to read/write — no resource-level granularity.
